name: Real: Waifu incorporating Gardevoir design elements
prompt:
A user wants to generate an image. Write the TypeScript code that implements this request:

'I have a picture of my original waifu character and a picture of Gardevoir (the Pokemon). I want the waifu to incorporate Gardevoir's design elements — the flowing green-and-white color palette, the elegant posture, the horn-like headpiece — but she must remain clearly herself. Do NOT turn her into Gardevoir. She should still look like the original waifu, just with those specific design touches.'

Your code should export a function `blendDesigns(subjectBase64: string, designRefBase64: string): Promise<string>` that calls Gemini and returns the generated image base64. Do NOT write a main() runner.

assertions.name: two_images
assertions.check: puts both images into the generation call

assertions.name: identity_guardrail
assertions.check: explicitly states NOT to turn subject into the reference

assertions.name: selective_copy
assertions.check: lists specific elements to copy (palette, posture, headpiece)

assertions.name: parts_order
assertions.test: (c) => { const refIdx = c.search(/designRefBase64|gardevoir/i); const subIdx = c.search(/subjectBase64|waifu/i); return refIdx >= 0 && subIdx >= 0 && refIdx < subIdx; }
